task_completion.description: Verify that 4 new critical third-party vendors have been registered, excluding AWS and Salesforce.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba's Third Party / Vendor management section showing a list of at least 4 newly added vendors. None of the vendors should be named 'AWS', 'Amazon Web Services', or 'Salesforce'.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least one risk is documented for each of the 4 registered vendors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba's Risk Management or Vendor details screens showing at least one associated risk entry linked to each of the 4 new vendors.

task_completion.description: Verify that a treatment strategy is documented for each of the identified vendor risks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Risk treatment section or risk details in Eramba showing a defined treatment strategy (e.g., mitigate, accept, transfer) for the documented vendor risks.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a security policy titled 'Third-Party Risk Management Policy' with an 'Approved' status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba's Policy Management section showing a policy exactly named 'Third-Party Risk Management Policy' with its status clearly marked as 'Approved'.

task_completion.description: Verify the establishment of a project that includes the word 'Vendor' in its title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba's Project Management section showing a newly created project with 'Vendor' included in the project title.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of 2 policy exceptions for vendors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eramba's Exceptions management section showing at least 2 policy exceptions created and linked to the vendors or TPRM policy.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the Eramba web interface rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Eramba web application loaded in a web browser with visible navigation, form submissions, and UI elements.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually filled out the forms in Eramba and didn't just create a mock text document or HTML page.
integrity.visual_evidence: Eramba interface elements (menus, breadcrumbs, data tables) should be visible and consistent with the application's standard layout, showing successful creation toasts or updated tables.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use terminal commands to directly insert records into the Eramba database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing SQL commands, database client interfaces, or curl API requests to bypass the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves a Security Management Specialist (ONET 13-1199.07) in the Banking / Financial Services industry. The 2023 MOVEit supply chain breach is a real event (CVE-2023-34362). First National Capital Bank is a synthetic organization created for this simulation. Legitimate banking vendors that might be used include FIS Global, Jack Henry & Associates, Experian, and Symitar.
